title: rubus × subamoenus
intro: Rubus × subamoenus, also known as the black raspberry, is a hybrid of the red raspberry and the black raspberry, characterized by its dark purple to black fruit and thorny canes. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its high antioxidant and anti-inflammatory content, which has been shown to have potential health benefits such as reducing cancer risk and improving cardiovascular health. The health benefits of Rubus × subamoenus include its ability to support digestive health, reduce inflammation, and act as an antimicrobial agent. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in teas, tinctures, and jams to take advantage of its medicinal properties, and it is also used in supplements and topical creams to support skin health and reduce inflammation.
